text: I work at Sucafina and I report to the director of operations.

Good morning, Walyson. Before your shift, here is a quick update: the warehouse inventory is ready and the forecast for this week is busy.
Thanks, Carla. I always check the spreadsheet first. How many shipments leave on Wednesday?
Three shipments leave on Wednesday, and the big batch for the German client has a deadline on Thursday.
Understood. I usually catch up on my emails by nine, so I will confirm every order this morning.
Perfect. The truck from the port sometimes arrives early, so we may receive the new delivery before lunch.
No problem. I never leave a shipment unchecked. I will wrap up the schedule and send you a summary.

Hi, this is Walyson. Let me tell you about a typical Monday at Sucafina. My alarm rings at five thirty, and my commute to the office in Santos usually takes about forty minutes. I always start my shift with a strong coffee, of course. The first thing I do is open my spreadsheet and check the numbers from the weekend. I often review the inventory in the warehouse, because we always need to know how much coffee we hold before the market opens. By nine, I usually catch up on my emails and confirm the shipments that leave this week. There is always a deadline, so I rarely have a quiet morning. Around noon, I check the forecast for prices and prepare the next batch for export. I sometimes join a quick call with our office in Geneva. I never finish the day without a clear plan for Tuesday, and I always wrap up by writing a short summary for my director.

Hi Walyson, this is Carla from the operations team. Here is a quick update before your Monday shift. The warehouse inventory was counted on Friday, and everything looks fine. The forecast for this week is busy: three shipments leave on Wednesday, and the big batch for our German client has a tight deadline on Thursday. I usually send the spreadsheet by eight, but today it will be ready a little later, so please do not worry. I often update the schedule during the day, so check it before the afternoon call. One more thing: the truck from the port sometimes arrives early, so we may need to receive the new delivery before lunch. I always confirm the numbers twice, and I have already double-checked them. Talk soon, and have a great start to the week.

Walyson wakes up early and commutes to the Sucafina office in Santos. He starts his shift with a coffee and opens his spreadsheet to check the numbers. In the morning, he reviews the warehouse inventory and catches up on his emails. Around noon, he checks the price forecast and prepares the next batch for export. At the end of the day, he wraps up by writing a short summary for his director.
